name: Nice and Easy Sautéed Zucchini, Summer Squash, and Cherry Tomatoes with Chilies and Herbs
recipeInstructions: There are many ways you can cook zucchini and summer squash, but all of them have to deal with the issue of excess moisture in the long run. The easiest way I know of is to cook it hard and fast (ok, "nice and rough," if you prefer) in a skillet with a bit of olive oil. The goal is to get the squashes to brown before their internal structure has a chance to break down too much and expel liquid.
A single layer in a cast iron skillet is a good way to do it. A wok would work as well.
Once they've browned, here comes the rough twist: a serrano chili finely minced up with a shallot tossed into the skillet with a handful of cherry tomatoes.

It's just enough heat to give your mouth a bit of a kick in the pants. Once the aromatics and tomatoes are added, it takes only about 30 seconds for them to soften up and coat the zucchini and squash with their flavor.
author: seriouseats.com
